intent_questions.question: What behavior must be proven?
intent_questions.self_answer: GET /health against the Dockerized Crawl4AI service must satisfy Docker curl -f semantics, meaning the observed HTTP status is below 400. The response body is not asserted.
intent_questions.risk_if_wrong: A weak model may invent an exact status/body contract that the selected public evidence does not provide.
intent_questions.how_to_override: Add exact public /health response status/body evidence and rerun the selected candidate checker.

intent_questions.question: What is the system boundary: Runner target, Mocker dependency, hook host, or configuration-as-code?
intent_questions.self_answer: The Runner target is an externally-started, test-owned Docker container exposing Crawl4AI on 127.0.0.1:11235. QaaS YAML performs only the HTTP transaction/assertion; the custom status assertion is a Runner hook and Docker lifecycle remains outside QaaS YAML.
intent_questions.risk_if_wrong: A weak model may put Docker lifecycle fields into undocumented QaaS YAML or remove a user-owned container.
intent_questions.how_to_override: Provide documented QaaS lifecycle support or keep Docker lifecycle in the external harness plan.

intent_questions.question: What public docs/schema path proves the capability exists?
intent_questions.self_answer: QaaS public docs/schema support HTTP Transactions, FromFileSystem payload data sources, and custom assertions by short type name; the custom assertion must still pass build/template/live validation.
intent_questions.risk_if_wrong: The YAML could cite a built-in range status assertion that does not exist.
intent_questions.how_to_override: Update docs evidence and rerun the selected candidate checker.

intent_questions.question: What inputs, outputs, and side effects prove success?
intent_questions.self_answer: Input is an empty GET request to /health. Output assertion is HTTP status less than 400 only. Body/schema and /crawl behavior are explicitly out of scope.
intent_questions.risk_if_wrong: A weak model may promote /crawl or response body assertions from non-deterministic README snippets.
intent_questions.how_to_override: Harvest deterministic public body/schema evidence before adding those assertions.

intent_questions.question: Which negative, malformed, outage, retry, cleanup, and observability cases matter?
intent_questions.self_answer: Negative, malformed, outage, retry, and observability cases remain blocked. Cleanup is explicitly required and must remove only a unique test-owned container. This candidate covers only the Docker /health happy path.
intent_questions.risk_if_wrong: A weak model may claim broad Crawl4AI API coverage or safe Docker cleanup from one healthcheck.
intent_questions.how_to_override: Add exact public negative/observability contracts and live cleanup evidence before expanding assertions.

intent_questions.question: Which dependencies must exist: broker, HTTP endpoint, database, Redis, S3, Kubernetes, filesystem, credentials, ports?
intent_questions.self_answer: Docker must be available, image pull must succeed, the HTTP endpoint must bind 127.0.0.1:11235 safely, Redis and browser shared memory requirements must be satisfied inside the container, and cleanup must remove only a unique test-owned container.
intent_questions.risk_if_wrong: The candidate may be promoted in an environment where Docker lifecycle is unsafe or unavailable.
intent_questions.how_to_override: Attach Docker lifecycle, readiness, cleanup, QaaS live, airgapped, and strong-review transcripts.

intent_questions.question: What can run now, and what must be deferred?
intent_questions.self_answer: Static selected-candidate validation can run now. Docker lifecycle/cleanup, custom assertion build/template/live validation, QaaS live act/assert, strong review, and live airgapped weak-model validation are deferred and block promotion.
intent_questions.risk_if_wrong: A weak model may confuse a blocked healthcheck candidate with an executable promoted test.
intent_questions.how_to_override: Attach passing Docker lifecycle, custom assertion build/template/live, QaaS live, cleanup, airgapped, and strong-review evidence.

intent_assumptions.assumption: Docker curl -f /health is represented by a custom HttpStatusBelow400 assertion, not by built-in HttpStatus equality.
intent_assumptions.why_safe: The public Dockerfile and compose healthchecks use curl -f, and public QaaS docs support custom assertions when built and template-validated.
intent_assumptions.risk_if_wrong: A weak model may assert StatusCode 200 or body content without selected evidence.
intent_assumptions.how_to_override: Provide exact public status/body evidence or a different documented assertion.

intent_assumptions.assumption: The /crawl README example is not promoted.
intent_assumptions.why_safe: The selected README branches on results versus task_id and lacks a deterministic response body contract.
intent_assumptions.risk_if_wrong: A weak model may turn a sample client snippet into an exact API assertion.
intent_assumptions.how_to_override: Harvest exact public /crawl request and response contracts before promotion.

assertions: Candidate Runner YAML uses a docs-derived custom HttpStatusBelow400 assertion for Docker curl -f semantics.
Candidate intentionally does not assert /health response body or schema.
Candidate intentionally does not promote /crawl from the README sample client.

dependency_gates.blocked_reason: The custom HttpStatusBelow400 assertion has not passed build/schema/template/live validation.
Docker pull/run/readiness with a unique test-owned container has not been live-validated.
Unique test-owned container cleanup has not been live-validated.
QaaS template validation has not run for this candidate.
Live QaaS run/act/assert has not run against a test-owned Crawl4AI container.
Live weak-model validation has not passed.

required_evidence: Public API, CLI, or runtime contract
C# build result when code artifacts exist
Docker pull/run/readiness transcript with unique container name
Docker cleanup transcript proving no user-owned crawl4ai container was removed
Live QaaS run/act/assert result when dependency gates are ready

cleanup: No live Docker cleanup was performed during static candidate generation.
validation_sequence: Validate selected immutable public Docker healthcheck evidence.
Validate QaaS public docs/schema fields and custom assertion plan.
Run Docker lifecycle validation with a unique test-owned container.
Build and template-validate the HttpStatusBelow400 assertion.
Run live QaaS act/assert against the tracked container.
Run live airgapped weak-model validation and strong review before promotion.

source_only_blockers.description: The README command and Docker healthcheck are public evidence, but Docker image pull, startup, readiness, resource limits, and cleanup have not been executed and verified.
required_evidence: Cleanup transcript proving only the test-owned container was removed
source_only_blockers.unblock_instruction: Run a Docker lifecycle harness that uses a unique container name and preserves any user-owned crawl4ai container.

source_only_blockers.description: A docs-derived HttpStatusBelow400 custom assertion is authored and referenced, but Runner assembly discovery, schema/template validation, build validation, and live QaaS act/assert have not passed.
source_only_blockers.unblock_instruction: Reference the assertion assembly, regenerate schema/clean bin, run QaaS template validation, then run live QaaS act/assert before promotion.

source_only_blockers.description: Selected public evidence defines Docker curl -f status semantics for /health, but no exact response body or schema contract.
required_evidence: Immutable public /health response body or schema evidence
source_only_blockers.unblock_instruction: Do not add body/schema assertions until deterministic public evidence is harvested.

source_only_blockers.description: The README /crawl sample branches between results and task_id polling; it is not a deterministic input/output contract.
source_only_blockers.unblock_instruction: Keep /crawl out of candidate YAML until exact response and side-effect evidence exists.

source_only_blockers.description: The Runner YAML and custom assertion are schema-derived but have not passed QaaS template or live act/assert validation.
source_only_blockers.unblock_instruction: Run documented QaaS validation commands in an environment with Runner host and test-owned Crawl4AI container.

source_only_blockers.description: Live weak-model validation has not passed for this candidate packet.
required_evidence: Airgapped transcript with dry_run false and /health status-only contract preserved
source_only_blockers.unblock_instruction: Run D:\QaaS\_tools\weak-model-session.ps1 -Airgapped -All -ReasoningEffort none when quota/routing is available.

blocked_reason: Selected public contracts support a Crawl4AI Docker /health status-only candidate, but executable promotion is blocked until Docker lifecycle/cleanup, custom assertion build/template/live, QaaS template/live, airgapped, and strong-review evidence pass.
